skill.name: learning-tutoring
skill.description: Learning tutoring planning and content production skill for creating study plans, generating exercises, writing answer explanations, and providing review/adjustment guidance; triggered by requests like study plan, exercise set/question bank, answer analysis, error analysis, exam prep plan, or spaced/periodic review schedule.
skill.summary: Generates structured study plans, practice exercises, answer explanations, and review guidance for any subject and learner level.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found 45 potential issues but all are false positives after evaluation. The 18 high-severity 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false positives triggered by f-strings and markdown text that resemble encoded data. The 23 medium-severity 'shell backtick execution' findings are false positives caused by markdown inline code formatting and code blocks that contain educational shell examples. The 6 low-severity 'system reconnaissance' findings are false positives from educational vocabulary. The 2 network findings are the legitimate GitHub source URL. The only confirmed finding is 2 benign file write operations in build_tutoring_pack.py which write a JSON output file to a hardcoded outputs/ directory. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or malicious patterns detected.

low_findings.title: Python file write operation for output generation
low_findings.description: build_tutoring_pack.py writes generated tutoring content to a JSON file. The output path is hardcoded in CONFIG as outputs/tutoring_pack.json. No user-controlled path injection is possible. This is a legitimate and essential function of the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Python file write operation for output generation
dangerous_patterns.description: build_tutoring_pack.py creates a directory and writes a JSON file. The output path is hardcoded in CONFIG as 'outputs/tutoring_pack.json' and is not derived from user input. This is a benign, expected behavior for a content generation skill.

content.user_title: Create study plans and practice exercises for any subject
content.value_statement: Students and tutors often struggle to create structured study plans with aligned practice materials. This skill generates complete tutoring packages including weekly study plans, practice questions with explanations, and review schedules tailored to any subject and learner level.
actual_capabilities: Generates multi-week study plans with weekly focus topics, tasks, and milestones
Creates practice questions in three formats: multiple choice, short answer, and application
Produces answer explanations with correct logic, common pitfalls, and transfer tips
Generates structured JSON output via the build_tutoring_pack.py script
Provides review and adjustment guidance with spaced repetition schedules
Supports any subject from programming to mathematics to language learning
limitations: Script output quality depends on the CONFIG values set before execution
Does not support interactive tutoring or real-time question answering
Generated content follows templates and may need manual refinement for advanced topics
The build script produces placeholder answers rather than verified correct solutions

use_cases.title: Self-study planning for students
use_cases.description: A student preparing for an exam can provide their subject, current level, and time budget. The skill generates a structured weekly plan with practice exercises and milestones.
use_cases.target_user: Students preparing for exams or mastering new subjects independently

use_cases.title: Tutor lesson preparation
use_cases.description: A tutor needs a structured curriculum and practice materials for their student. The skill produces a complete study plan with exercises organized by difficulty and topic.
use_cases.target_user: Tutors and teachers creating structured learning materials

use_cases.title: Coding bootcamp curriculum design
use_cases.description: An instructor designing a programming course can use this skill to generate a multi-week plan covering Python basics with exercises, projects, and review checkpoints.
use_cases.target_user: Curriculum designers and coding bootcamp instructors

prompt_templates.title: Create a study plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 6-week study plan for high-school algebra focusing on functions. I can study 6 hours per week. My goal is to score 85 or higher on a school exam in 7 weeks. I prefer practice questions and concise notes.
prompt_templates.scenario: A student asks for a structured study plan with weekly breakdown, milestones, and review schedule

prompt_templates.title: Generate practice exercises
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate 10 practice questions about Python lists and dictionaries. Include 4 multiple choice, 4 short answer, and 2 application problems. Provide answers and explanations for each question.
prompt_templates.scenario: A learner needs practice materials for a specific topic with mixed question types

prompt_templates.title: Answer analysis and explanation
prompt_templates.prompt: I got the following question wrong: 'What is the output of print(type([]))?' I answered 'list'. Please explain the correct answer, common pitfalls, and how to think about this type of question in general.
prompt_templates.scenario: A student provides a mistake and asks for diagnosis, correct reasoning, and remediation guidance

prompt_templates.title: Exam preparation with script execution
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a complete tutoring pack for Python basics. The student is a beginner with zero foundation. Duration is 4 weeks at 4 hours per week. Fill in the CONFIG in build_tutoring_pack.py and run the script to generate the full package.
prompt_templates.scenario: An advanced user wants to use the build script for batch generation of structured tutoring materials

output_examples.input: Create a 4-week study plan for Python basics for a complete beginner with 4 hours per week.
output: Learner Profile: Python basics / Zero foundation / 4 hours per week
Goals: Master variables, conditions, loops, and functions. Complete simple data processing tasks.
Week 1: Variables and data types. Tasks include reading comprehension and hands-on exercises.
Week 2: Conditional judgment and loops. Practice with if-else patterns and for/while loops.
Week 3: Functions and parameters. Build reusable code blocks with parameters and return values.
Week 4: Lists, dictionaries, and file I/O. Combine all concepts in a final project.

output_examples.input: Generate 3 practice questions about Python functions with answers and explanations.
output: 1) Type: choice. Question: Which keyword is used to define a function in Python? Answer: def. Explanation: Python uses the def keyword followed by the function name and parentheses.

2) Type: short_answer. Question: Explain the difference between a parameter and an argument. Answer: A parameter is the variable listed in the function definition. An argument is the value passed to the function when calling it.
3) Type: application. Question: Write a function that takes a list of numbers and returns their average. Explanation: Sum all elements and divide by the list length. Handle empty lists with a check.

best_practices: Always clarify the learner's current level, weekly time budget, and specific goals before generating a plan.
Review the generated exercises for correctness before sharing, since placeholder answers are template-based.
Use the build_tutoring_pack.py script for batch generation when you need consistent JSON output across multiple topics.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for real-time interactive tutoring or live question answering it is designed for structured content generation only.
Do not skip the learner profiling step generating a plan without knowing the learner's level and goals will produce generic content.
Do not use the build script output as final verified content the script generates template-based placeholder answers that need human review.

faq.question: What subjects does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports any subject. You can specify the topic and the skill will generate a study plan and exercises tailored to that subject.

faq.question: Do I need to run the Python script to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. The skill can generate plans and exercises directly. The build_tutoring_pack.py script is optional for users who want structured JSON output.

faq.question: Are the generated answers always correct?
faq.answer: No. The Python script generates template-based placeholder answers. The markdown workflow produces better explanations but all output should be reviewed for accuracy.

faq.question: How do I customize the study plan for my needs?
faq.answer: Tell the skill your subject, current level, weekly time budget, goals, and preferred learning style. The skill uses this profile to generate a tailored plan.

faq.question: Can this skill generate questions in languages other than English?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can generate study plans and exercises in multiple languages. Specify your preferred language when describing the request.

faq.question: Does this skill store or share my learning data?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates content locally within the AI session. The build script writes output to a local file on your machine.
